items.headline: Trump tells Congress "hostilities" with Iran have "terminated"
items.summary: President Trump on Friday told Congress that hostilities with Iran have "terminated," addressing a critical 60-day deadline.

items.headline: Trump Claims It’s “Unconstitutional” for Congress to Keep Him in Check
items.summary: President Donald Trump claimed Friday it’s unconstitutional to seek congressional approval for war. Speaking to the press outside the White House, Trump whined that he should not have to comply with the War Powers Resolution of 1973, which requires the presid…

items.headline: White House Says Iran Hostilities ‘Terminated’ as War Powers Deadline Arrives
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump's administration argued that a ceasefire with Tehran had "terminated" hostilities as a legal deadline arrived on Friday for coming to Congress about the two-month Iran war.

items.headline: UAE says Iran cannot be trusted over Strait of Hormuz as peace efforts remain deadlocked
items.summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has caused unprecedented disruption to energy markets, choking off 20% of the world's oil and gas supplies and causing a record rally in oil prices.

items.headline: Top CEO pay increased 20 times faster than workers’ pay in 2025
items.summary:
• Global real worker pay fell 12 percent while real CEO pay surged 54 percent between 2019 and 2025.
  • At least four CEOs of major corporations each pocketed over $100 million in pay and bonuses last year. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan led the pack at ove…

items.headline: Donald Trump's 'wishful thinking' over his blockade of Iranian oil
items.summary: Analysis of how much oil Iran is producing, shipping and having to store shows pressure is mounting on Tehran, but while experts agree the US blockade is effective, there are questions about whether it can really force Iran back to the negotiating table.

items.headline: Only Iran-Linked Traffic Moves Through Hormuz Amid Blockades
items.summary: A few Iran-linked vessels are making their way through the Strait of Hormuz, as the crucial waterway remains effectively closed to most international shipping amid blockades by both Tehran and the US. Just a single Iran-linked fuel tanker was observed …

items.headline: Iranian Oil Smuggling Scheme Uses Fake Iraqi Ship Identities to Evade U.S. Blockade, Intelligence Firm Says
items.summary: A maritime intelligence firm reported on April 29 that a group of U.S.-sanctioned oil tankers are falsifying their location data to appear anchored off Iraq while secretly loading Iranian crude, according to Windward AI. The scheme involves four very large cr…

items.headline: Trump admin pitches new international coalition to coordinate reopening of Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The Maritime Freedom Construct is meant to be a diplomatic and military push to work with allied countries and shipping companies to help reopen the vital waterway.

items.headline: Iran Threatens Painful Response if US Resumes Attacks, Oil Prices Seesaw
items.summary: Iran said on Thursday it would respond with “long and painful strikes” on U.S. positions if Washington renewed attacks, and also reasserted its control over the Strait of Hormuz, complicating U.S. plans for a coalition to reopen the waterway. Two …

items.headline: Has the US-Iran ceasefire reset the clock on War Powers Act deadline?
items.summary: Defence secretary claims hostilities have ceased, but lawmakers say US forces remain active despite halted air raids.

items.headline: The 60-day War Powers deadline is up on Friday. What could happen next?
items.summary: As Trump continues to keep a military presence in the Middle East, the 60-day deadline for the administration to seek congressional authorization is up on Friday.

items.headline: Democrats fail to shatter Republicans' resolve on eve of crucial Iran deadline
items.summary: Senate Republicans reject Democrats' sixth attempt to limit President Donald Trump's authority in Iran as a critical 60-day war powers deadline arrives on Friday.

items.headline: Pete Hegseth heads to Capitol Hill to defend Trump's Iran war as 60-day congressional deadline looms
items.summary: Department of War Secretary Pete Hegseth is set to testify before House Armed Services Committee to defend the administration's military campaign in Iran.

items.headline: Dem plot to limit Trump war powers on Cuba fails as GOP falls in line with military action abroad
items.summary: Republicans blocked Senate Democrats' war powers resolution seeking to prevent President Donald Trump from taking military action against Cuba without Congress' approval.

items.headline: "Political Disaster for Donald Trump": Jeremy Scahill on Stalled U.S.-Iran Talks

items.headline: You Are Not Ready For Trump And Iran And Whatever The F*ck THIS Was
items.summary: It's gonna explode any minute now!

items.headline: China is Equal In Any Future Fight, General Warns...
items.summary: Faced with the rapidly growing China threat, the U.S. has to start hardening its bases now, said Marine Lt. Gen. Stephen Sklenka.

items.headline: The American Military Is Blowing Its Load In Iran — And China Is Noticing
items.summary: As the U.S. military continuously depletes its assets in the war on Iran, China is taking notes.

items.headline: Iran Tensions Send WTI Crude Surging Above $105
items.summary: June WTI crude oil futures surged this week, with the contract trading at $105.88 Thursday night, up $11.48 or 12.16%. The weekly high is $110.93, while the low is $94.59. With Friday still ahead, the market has not closed the week yet, but the move has alrea…

items.headline: Britain is more exposed to jet fuel shortages than other European countries, experts warn amid fears supply crisis could spark a summer of travel disruption
items.summary: The UK has the lowest deficit - when imported fuel is subtracted from the amount made at home - of any major economy in the continent, according to research by Allianz.

items.headline: Soaring food and petrol prices... and SIX interest rate hikes plunging the UK towards recession: BoE leads dire warnings over 'Trumpflation' risks - with no end in sight to Iran chaos
items.summary: Brits face the possibility of six interest rate rises over the coming year as policymakers battle to contain inflation - which they fear could nearly double to 6.2 per cent.

items.headline: US yield surge piles fresh pressure on India bonds already bruised by oil shock
items.summary: The Indian 6.48% 2035 bond yield is expected to range between 6.98% and 7.05%, following a close of 6.9928% on Wednesday, according to a private-bank trader

items.headline: W.O.R.R is the worry. A four-way assault pummells Indian macro
items.summary: India faces a rare convergence of four macro shocks—war-driven oil surge, a record weak rupee, and a likely poor monsoon—raising inflation and growth risks. The Finance Ministry warns these interacting pressures could strain external balances and policy choic…

items.headline: Trump is trying to negotiate with an Iranian regime at war with itself
items.summary: Tehran's power structure shows signs of collapse as IRGC, parliamentary and security factions clash amid rumors surrounding Mojtaba Khamenei.

items.headline: Iran nuclear shake-up: Hardliner poised to seize control of U.S. talks after Ghalibaf forced out
items.summary: Iran’s nuclear negotiating team is undergoing a major shakeup as the head of the delegation steps down after attempting to discuss the nuclear issue with the U.S. Hardliner Saeed Jalili is expected to replace Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf as Iran’s lead nuclear ne…

items.headline: Iran turmoil erupts: Ultra-hardliner who mocked Trump poised to take over nuclear talks
items.summary: Reports say veteran hardliner Saeed Jalili may replace Ghalibaf on Iran's negotiating team, deepening fractures within the regime's political leadership.

items.headline: Humanity may be doomed to die in nuclear war—unless we act soon, physicist David Gross says
items.summary: After winning a Breakthrough Prize, the world’s most lucrative science award, theoretical physicist David Gross is using the moment to warn of nuclear war’s existential threat—and how we can escape it

items.headline: Crunch nuclear proliferation meeting at UN amid raging global wars
items.summary: Signatories of the landmark nuclear non-proliferation treaty will meet at the UN from Monday as hopes fade they can reach agreement and tensions soar between the atomic powers.  In 2022, during the last review of the treaty that is considered the cornerstone …

items.headline: Ukraine plans army pay hikes, phased discharge — Zelenskyy
items.summary: The Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced reforms to boost morale and address military complaints about exhaustion and uneven workloads on the frontline. Ukraine plans to raise military wages, improve contracts and implement a phased discharge sys…

items.headline: Ukraine Hits Russian Black Sea Oil Port Four Times in One Week
items.summary: Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse was targeted and hit by a new Ukrainian drone strike early on Friday, in the fourth attack on the port in just one week as Ukraine looks to limit Russia's oil exports and revenues amid soaring crude prices. Since internationa…

items.headline: Russian Port City Fights Fire After Fourth Strike in Weeks
items.summary: Russian officials said a fire broke out at a terminal in Russia’s Black Sea port of Tuapse after an attack by Ukrainian drones on Friday, the fourth such strike in just over two weeks.

items.headline: Ukraine begins to flex muscle as an emerging air power, angering Russia
items.summary: Russian officials cast Ukrainian attacks as 'terrorism' as they urge 'vigilance'.
